plugin_desc: QTech (QSW)-specific SNMP collection for switches (QTECH-MIB, enterprise 27514). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects QTech private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization, system memory (used/total), temperature, fan and power-supply status. Memory utilization is computed from used/total; fan/psu states (native 0=normal) are normalized to the unified 1=normal convention. Supported brands/models — QTech QSW-series switches.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the QTech (QSW) device control plane (QTECH-MIB), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the QTech (QSW) system memory (QTECH-MIB). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the QTech (QSW) system memory (QTECH-MIB). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the QTech (QSW) device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the testpoint temperature of the QTech (QSW) device (QTECH-MIB switchTemperature), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each QTech (QSW) cooling fan (QTECH-MIB sysFanStatus), normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state indicates a cooling fault that can quickly lead to overheating and should be investigated immediately.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each QTech (QSW) power supply unit (QTECH-MIB sysPowerStatus), normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state signals a power redundancy or hardware fault and must be remediated promptly.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
